content: I'm planning a trip to Japan. What are must-see places?
Tokyo, Kyoto, Osaka, and Hiroshima are top picks. Don't miss Fushimi Inari shrine, the bamboo groves of Arashiyama, and the teamLab digital art museums.
What's the best time of year to go?
Spring (late March–early May) for cherry blossoms and autumn (October–November) for fall foliage are the most popular. Summer is hot and humid; winter is cold but less crowded.
Do I need to speak Japanese?
Not necessarily. Major tourist areas have English signage and many locals in hospitality speak basic English. A translation app on your phone goes a long way.
